title: corylus maxima f. atropurpurea
intro: Corylus maxima f. atropurpurea, also known as Purple Filbert or Purple Hazelnut, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Betulaceae, characterized by its deep purple foliage and edible nuts. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. The nuts and leaves of the Purple Filbert have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
